title: Moms Id Like To Forget

jokes.text: Ooh!
jokes.punchline: Brief exclamation suggesting surprise or pain
jokes.explanation: Standard opening reaction sound - economical but minimal comedic value

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.punchline: The iconic frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Homer's iconic catchphrase - perfect character integration, culturally significant

jokes.text: We're down to our final four huckers!
jokes.punchline: Use of 'huckers' as dodgeball players
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay on dodgeball terminology

jokes.text: Three wily fourth graders and one scrappy fifth gradesman! To the death!
jokes.punchline: Escalates to gladiator-style 'to the death' commentary
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from normal sports commentary to absurd death match

jokes.text: Where do you want your ball mark, face or belly? How about... in your dreams! Hee-yah!
jokes.setup: Student offers targeting choices to opponent
jokes.punchline: Reverses with confident rejection and attack cry
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection with kid-appropriate trash talk

jokes.text: I'm not the coach! There is no coach! I just wanted to see if you were wearing a bra.
jokes.setup: Student appears to be giving coaching advice
jokes.punchline: Reveals creepy ulterior motive
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable humor typical of schoolyard dynamics

jokes.text: Your four eyes saw a lot today, Milhouse. What's the skinny?
jokes.setup: Bart treating Milhouse as sports reporter
jokes.punchline: Reference to Milhouse's glasses as 'four eyes'
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart-Milhouse dynamic with mild insult

jokes.text: The fifth graders played a great game. They got a great program up there. Great fans! But this was just our day.
jokes.punchline: Generic sports interview clichés from elementary student
jokes.explanation: Good satire of sports interview clichés delivered by kid

jokes.text: I only took this refereeing job 'cause I was cold and needed a shirt!
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's only doing it for basic clothing needs
jokes.explanation: Perfect Willie character moment - absurd motivation delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: I deem this victory Pyrrhic! ALL Pyrrhic! Pyrrhic! Your response is puerile! ALL Puerile! Puerile!
jokes.punchline: Other kids mindlessly repeat big words they don't understand
jokes.explanation: Classic Martin pompousness with good group dynamic comedy

jokes.text: There goes the fifth grade, floatin' down the Delaware, chewin' on their underwear, can't afford another pair. Ten days later, bitten by a polar bear. That's how the polar bear died.
jokes.setup: Kids singing insulting song about fifth graders
jokes.punchline: Escalates to absurd polar bear death twist
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation and misdirection - starts as schoolyard taunt, ends with absurd reversal

jokes.text: How dare they dishonor the lyrics of 'Old Gray Mare'! How dare they!
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top indignation about children's song parody
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Edna's priorities

jokes.text: I like to think my kids would know how to lose gracefully. In the fifth grade, we teach character. Mike, let's be adults here. You're right, Edna. Fourth sucks. Oh, that's it!
jokes.setup: Teachers trying to model mature behavior
jokes.punchline: Immediately devolves into childish insults
jokes.explanation: Nice setup/payoff showing adult hypocrisy

jokes.text: Hey, fourth graders, can you help us with our fractions? Oh, wait, you can't. They ain't even read Where the Red Fern Grows yet.
jokes.punchline: Academic superiority insults about basic curriculum
jokes.explanation: Realistic kid insults based on grade-level differences

jokes.text: A real rumble, just like in that movie where the Jets fought the Sharks. Hmm...
jokes.punchline: Other kids' confused silence/looks show they don't get the reference
jokes.explanation: Cultural reference gap between kid and peers

jokes.text: Anybody want to wait for shine? Yeah. Yeah. Yeah. Yeah. Definitely shine.
jokes.setup: Tough kids challenging each other to rumble
jokes.punchline: Immediately want to postpone for better weather
jokes.explanation: Great subversion of tough kid posturing - they're still just kids

jokes.text: no biting, no throwing rocks, no face farts, flying face farts, backdoor haircuts, 'nad noogies, moist Michaels, and absolutely no 'Brazilian hardwood.'
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd and inappropriate prohibited moves
jokes.explanation: Creative list of made-up fighting moves with escalating absurdity

jokes.text: Is this a rumble or a harvest dance? Okay, you want hardwood? No, no, no, no.
jokes.punchline: Others immediately back down when 'hardwood' is offered
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the rules setup - shows kids are all bluster

jokes.text: Did you wear those same clothes yesterday? No. I have two identical sets of these clothes. I see. With a stain on the shirt in the exact same place?
jokes.punchline: Student's obvious lie exposed by identical stain placement
jokes.explanation: Realistic awkward teacher-student interaction

jokes.text: Things aren't good at home.
jokes.setup: Student caught in lie about hygiene
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact admission of family problems
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with perfect understatement

jokes.text: Rumble! Why don't you head on home, pal. Bless you.
jokes.punchline: Another kid thinks it was a sneeze and says 'bless you'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on 'rumble' sounding like a sneeze

jokes.text: You call that a scar?! This is a scar! That's your belly button. Everybody's got one. I thought I was special.
jokes.setup: Nelson trying to show off his scar
jokes.punchline: Reveals he thought his belly button was a special scar
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson moment - tough kid revealed as clueless about basic anatomy

jokes.text: January 9, whaled on Jimbo with this book. Awaiting results. Unqualified success.
jokes.punchline: Formal documentation of bullying with professional language
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character detail - Nelson treating bullying like a business

jokes.text: Why do you have to eat peanuts in the shower? Can't start the day without that fresh-from-the-circus feeling.
jokes.setup: Marge questions Homer's odd shower habit
jokes.punchline: Homer's bizarrely specific and illogical explanation
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Homer logic - completely absurd but delivered with total confidence

jokes.text: Ew! You're sitting on the toilet! When it's closed, it's a chair.
jokes.punchline: Marge's practical justification
jokes.explanation: Relatable family moment with practical mom logic

jokes.text: You don't look like a mom, you look happy.
jokes.setup: Bart looking at old photo of Marge
jokes.punchline: Implies moms can't be happy
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about motherhood with dark undertone

jokes.text: We called ourselves the 'Cool Moms.' There's nothing cooler than calling yourself cool.
jokes.punchline: Narrator's sarcastic commentary on self-proclaimed coolness
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on the inherent uncoolness of claiming coolness

jokes.text: You can't buy white wine! Why not? Are you having red meat? Oh! Are you through with the chair?
jokes.punchline: Thinks it's about food pairing, then wants toilet seat
jokes.explanation: Callbacks toilet chair joke while showing Bart's misunderstanding

jokes.text: Been a while since our wives got together, huh? Mm. Whatever. Why are we getting together again? I was so happy when it stopped.
jokes.punchline: Honest admission they prefer when wives weren't friends
jokes.explanation: Realistic husband behavior - honest but not politically correct

jokes.text: No, that page isn't optimized for cell phone use... Why am I talking to you?! Because you're my best friend in the room.
jokes.setup: Homer frustrated with technology and conversation
jokes.punchline: Settles for 'best friend in the room' as low bar
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing forced male bonding

jokes.text: Check out my gnarly backwards foot!
jokes.punchline: Kid proud of injury/deformity
jokes.explanation: Basic kid humor about showing off injuries

jokes.text: Anyone see a good beer commercial lately? Asinine. Everything you say is asinine.
jokes.punchline: Brutal honest response to forced small talk
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable truth about forced male socializing

jokes.text: Stupid Flanders' Wi-Fi. Uh, that wasn't the Wi-Fi. My Bell's palsy's acting up.
jokes.setup: Homer blames technology for video chat problems
jokes.punchline: Other dad reveals it's actually his medical condition
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about medical condition mistaken for tech issues

jokes.text: Go out on a Tuesday? Who am I, Charlie Sheen?
jokes.setup: Marge asked to go out on weeknight
jokes.punchline: References Charlie Sheen's party reputation
jokes.explanation: Dated pop culture reference without much twist

jokes.text: Sand makes me sad because it used to be big rocks. But not anymore. Not anymore...
jokes.punchline: Melancholy observation about geological time
jokes.explanation: Beautiful absurdist moment - drunk Marge having existential thoughts about erosion

jokes.text: Kind of weird, us all being naked in here. We're not naked. Right. Neither am I.
jokes.setup: Kids in what's presumably a changing situation
jokes.punchline: Awkward admission about nudity
jokes.explanation: Mild awkward humor about kids and body awareness

jokes.text: They dared me to see how many TV remotes I could stick in my mouth.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous dare involving multiple remotes
jokes.explanation: Typical Bart accepting ridiculous peer pressure

jokes.text: Tell him it's the worst prognosis ever.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking to deliver message to Comic Book Guy
jokes.punchline: Doctor's inappropriately casual delivery of bad news
jokes.explanation: Dr. Hibbert's inappropriate bedside manner

jokes.text: Although frankly, I thought it would have been a long time ago. You are very uncurious about your body.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy knowing about Bart's scar origin
jokes.punchline: Insulting Bart's lack of self-awareness
jokes.explanation: Good Comic Book Guy condescension, fits character perfectly

jokes.text: I can say it in Na'vi or Klingon, which are pretty much the same.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy refusing to help
jokes.punchline: Dismissive comment about fictional languages
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy superiority and nerd gatekeeping

jokes.text: Our story begins with the creation of Middle Earth, as recounted in the Ainulindal and the Valaquenta. Behold Melkor, proudest of the Ainu!
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy about to tell simple story
jokes.punchline: Starts with elaborate Tolkien mythology
jokes.explanation: Classic Comic Book Guy over-complication and showing off

jokes.text: Fine. Children just want to know what is and not why it is.
jokes.punchline: Dismissive comment about children's intellectual curiosity
jokes.explanation: Smart observation about generational differences in curiosity

jokes.text: Your scar, like Tom Cruise's last good movie, was born on the Fourth of July.
jokes.punchline: References Tom Cruise movie while implying his recent work is bad
jokes.explanation: Clever pop culture reference with embedded criticism

jokes.text: It was seven years ago, a time when 'twilight' meant the end of the day and not the most barftastic horror franchise of all time.
jokes.punchline: Dismissive comment about Twilight franchise
jokes.explanation: Typical Comic Book Guy pop culture snobbery

jokes.text: a time when 'twilight' meant the end of the day and not the most barftastic horror franchise of all time.
jokes.punchline: Disparaging the Twilight franchise while dating the flashback
jokes.explanation: Good topical reference with typical Comic Book Guy disdain

jokes.text: Even though most of this town collaborated with the British, and we are still in informal talks to return to their empire, we all love fireworks!
jokes.punchline: Admits town's unpatriotic past and present
jokes.explanation: Good Quimby moment - accidentally admitting embarrassing truths

jokes.text: Traditionally, the Fourth of July fireworks were operated by the person most likely to be sober and dateless. Once again, the honor fell to me.
jokes.punchline: Self-aware admission of his social status
jokes.explanation: Rare moment of Comic Book Guy self-awareness about his social status

jokes.text: This is your TV for the year, kids. And the embers is your dinner.
jokes.punchline: Confused about what he's doing with fire and food
jokes.explanation: Homer's typical confusion about basic concepts

jokes.text: God, how I miss you.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy lamenting about Fourth of July
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's mourning his destroyed collectible/equipment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Comic Book Guy's priorities

jokes.text: I made you a coffee mug on Mother's Day! Isn't that enough? It's close, but no.
jokes.setup: Bart thinking his gift should be sufficient
jokes.punchline: Marge's diplomatic but honest response
jokes.explanation: Good family dynamic - realistic mother-son exchange

jokes.text: Your son always was the bad apple of the bunch. You know nothing about my son and even less about apples.
jokes.punchline: Marge's defensive comeback about apples
jokes.explanation: Good Marge moment - defending her son with unexpected apple knowledge dig

jokes.text: Maybe true friends aren't random people you meet at a Mommy-and-Me class. They're random people you meet in a college dorm.
jokes.punchline: Points out both situations are equally random
jokes.explanation: Smart observation about the arbitrary nature of friendship formation

jokes.text: Oh, you beautiful man! Oh... that feeling is Mutual of Omaha.
jokes.punchline: References insurance company inexplicably
jokes.explanation: Random Homer non-sequitur with insurance company reference

tmdb_overview: Marge reconnects with a close group of mothers she used to be friends with; Bart tries to break up their group when his playdates stop being fun.
